perturbation.original_text: 1. Confidential Information. Member shall not, during the term of the Franchise Agreement or thereafter, communicate, divulge or use, for any purpose other than the operation of the Franchised Business, any confidential information, knowledge, trade secrets or know-how which may be communicated to Member or which Member may learn by virtue of Member's relationship with Franchisee. All information, knowledge and know-how relating to Franchisor, its business plans, Franchised Businesses, or the System ("Confidential Information") is deemed confidential, except for information that Member can demonstrate came to Member's attention by lawful means prior to disclosure to Member; or which, at the time of the disclosure to Member, had become a part of the public domain.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Confidential Information. Member shall not, during the term of the Franchise Agreement or thereafter, communicate, divulge or use, for any purpose other than the operation of the Franchised Business, any confidential information, knowledge, trade secrets or know-how which may be communicated to Member or which Member may learn by virtue of Member's relationship with Franchisee. All information, knowledge and know-how relating to Franchisor, its business plans, Franchised Businesses, or the System ("Confidential Information") is deemed confidential. Information that Member can demonstrate came to Member's attention by lawful means prior to disclosure to Member is not considered confidential, but may still be subject to non-compete clauses. Also, information which, at the time of the disclosure to Member, had become a part of the public domain is deemed confidential.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the exceptions to the definition of "Confidential Information". Originally, information that was publicly available or known to the member prior to disclosure was not considered confidential. The altered version deems information that is publicly known, as confidential, which directly contradicts the original exception, creating uncertainty about what constitutes protected confidential information. Additionally, it states information known to the member prior to disclosure 'is not considered confidential, but may still be subject to non-compete clauses' which implies such information is both confidential and non-confidential at the same time, hence an In-Text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Confidential Information. Member shall not, during the term of the Franchise Agreement or thereafter, communicate, divulge or use, for any purpose other than the operation of the Franchised Business, any confidential information, knowledge, trade secrets or know-how which may be communicated to Member or which Member may learn by virtue of Member's relationship with Franchisee. All information, knowledge and know-how relating to Franchisor, its business plans, Franchised Businesses, or the System ("Confidential Information") is deemed confidential. Information that Member can demonstrate came to Member's attention by lawful means prior to disclosure to Member is not considered confidential, but may still be subject to non-compete clauses. Also, information which, at the time of the disclosure to Member, had become a part of the public domain is deemed confidential.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct in-text contradiction. The original text states that information in the public domain is *not* considered confidential. The changed text explicitly states that information in the public domain *is* considered confidential, which directly contradicts the original exclusion, creating a clear conflict within the definition of "Confidential Information", as well as introducing additional ambiguity about the non-compete clause.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Covenants Not to Compete. (a) Member specifically acknowledges that, pursuant to the Franchise Agreement, and by virtue of its position with Franchisee, Member will receive valuable specialized training and Confidential Information, including, without limitation, information regarding the operational, sales, promotional, and marketing methods and techniques of Franchisor and the System. (b) Member covenants and agrees that during the term of the Franchise Agreement, except as otherwise approved in writing by Franchisor, Member shall not, either directly or indirectly, for itself, or through, on behalf of, or in conjunction with any person, persons, partnership, corporation, or entity: (i) Own, manage, engage in, be employed by, advise, make loans to, consult for, rent or lease to, or have any other interest in business that (directly or indirectly) operates, or grants franchises or licenses to operate, a restaurant featuring pizza and related food specialties or that offers products or services substantially similar to those then offered by Pizza Fusions Restaurants ("Competitive Business"); C - 1 Source: PF HOSPITALITY GROUP INC., 10-12G, 9/23/2015 (ii) Divert or attempt to divert any business or customer, or potential business or customer, to any Competitive Business; or (iii) Induce any person to leave his or her employment with Franchisee or Franchisor. (c) Member covenants and agrees that during the Post-Term Period (defined below), except as otherwise approved in writing by Franchisor, Member shall not, either directly or indirectly, own, manage, engage in, be employed by, advise, make loans to, consult for, or have any other interest in any Competitive Business that is, or intends to operate, within a three (3) mile radius of the premises of your Franchised Business or within a three (3) mile radius of any Franchised Business then-operating or under construction to operate under the System.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Covenants Not to Compete. (a) Member specifically acknowledges that, pursuant to the Franchise Agreement, and by virtue of its position with Franchisee, Member may receive valuable specialized training and Confidential Information, including, without limitation, information regarding the operational, sales, promotional, and marketing methods and techniques of Franchisor and the System. (b) Member covenants and agrees that during the term of the Franchise Agreement, except as otherwise approved in writing by Franchisee, Member shall not, either directly or indirectly, for itself, or through, on behalf of, or in conjunction with any person, persons, partnership, corporation, or entity: (i) Own, manage, engage in, be employed by, advise, make loans to, consult for, rent or lease to, or have any other interest in business that (directly or indirectly) operates, or grants franchises or licenses to operate, a restaurant featuring pizza and related food specialties or that offers products or services substantially similar to those then offered by Pizza Fusions Restaurants ("Competitive Business"); C - 1 Source: PF HOSPITALITY GROUP INC., 10-12G, 9/23/2015 (ii) Divert or attempt to divert any business or customer, or potential business or customer, to any Competitive Business; or (iii) Induce any person to leave his or her employment with Franchisee or Franchisor. (c) Member covenants and agrees that during the Post-Term Period (defined below), except as otherwise approved in writing by Member, Member shall not, either directly or indirectly, own, manage, engage in, be employed by, advise, make loans to, consult for, or have any other interest in any Competitive Business that is, or intends to operate, within a three (3) mile radius of the premises of your Franchised Business or within a three (3) mile radius of any Franchised Business then-operating or under construction to operate under the System.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces in-text contradictions related to the non-compete covenants. First, in section (a), "will receive" is changed to "may receive", creating uncertainty about whether the member will actually receive training and confidential information, which weakens the justification for the non-compete agreement. Because the training and information is deemed to be valuable, it is assumed that the training will occur and should not be changed to "may receive". Second, in section (b), the requirement for written approval from "Franchisor" to engage in competitive activities is changed to "Franchisee". In section (c) written approval from "Franchisor" to engage in competitive activities is changed to "Member" which is illogical as it doesn't make sense for a member to approve themselves to do something. This contradicts the fundamental purpose of the non-compete clause, which is to protect the Franchisor's interests, not to allow the Franchisee or the Member decide whether they may compete against the Franchisor.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Covenants Not to Compete. (a) Member specifically acknowledges that, pursuant to the Franchise Agreement, and by virtue of its position with Franchisee, Member may receive valuable specialized training and Confidential Information, including, without limitation, information regarding the operational, sales, promotional, and marketing methods and techniques of Franchisor and the System. (b) Member covenants and agrees that during the term of the Franchise Agreement, except as otherwise approved in writing by Franchisee, Member shall not, either directly or indirectly, for itself, or through, on behalf of, or in conjunction with any person, persons, partnership, corporation, or entity: (i) Own, manage, engage in, be employed by, advise, make loans to, consult for, rent or lease to, or have any other interest in business that (directly or indirectly) operates, or grants franchises or licenses to operate, a restaurant featuring pizza and related food specialties or that offers products or services substantially similar to those then offered by Pizza Fusions Restaurants ("Competitive Business"); C - 1 Source: PF HOSPITALITY GROUP INC., 10-12G, 9/23/2015 (ii) Divert or attempt to divert any business or customer, or potential business or customer, to any Competitive Business; or (iii) Induce any person to leave his or her employment with Franchisee or Franchisor. (c) Member covenants and agrees that during the Post-Term Period (defined below), except as otherwise approved in writing by Member, Member shall not, either directly or indirectly, own, manage, engage in, be employed by, advise, make loans to, consult for, or have any other interest in any Competitive Business that is, or intends to operate, within a three (3) mile radius of the premises of your Franchised Business or within a three (3) mile radius of any Franchised Business then-operating or under construction to operate under the System.
perturbation.justification: The changes introduce strong in-text contradictions. First, changing 'will receive' to 'may receive' regarding valuable training and confidential information (2.(a)) undermines the justification for the non-compete agreement, creating uncertainty. Second, altering the approval authority from 'Franchisor' to 'Franchisee' in (2.(b)) and to 'Member' in (2.(c)) for engaging in competitive activities directly contradicts the purpose of a non-compete, which is to protect the Franchisor's interests. The contradiction arises because the contradicted text aims to restrict the franchisee from competing against the franchisor but is modified to allow the franchisee to authorize their own competitive activities.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Severability. All agreements and covenants contained herein are severable. If any of them, or any part or parts of them, shall be held invalid by any court of competent jurisdiction for any reason, then the Member agrees that the court shall have the authority to reform and modify that provision in order that the restriction shall be the maximum necessary to protect Franchisor's and/or Franchisee's legitimate business needs as permitted by applicable law and public policy. In so doing, the Member agrees that the court shall impose the provision with retroactive effect as close as possible to the provision held to be invalid.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Severability. All agreements and covenants contained herein are severable. If any of them, or any part or parts of them, shall be held invalid by any court of competent jurisdiction for any reason, then the Member agrees that the court shall have the authority to reform and modify that provision at their discretion. In so doing, the Member agrees that the court shall impose the provision with retroactive effect if needed.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction within the severability clause. The original clause allows the court to reform and modify invalid provisions to protect the Franchisor's and Franchisee's legitimate business needs. By changing the wording to allow the court to reform and modify that provision at "their discretion", without needing to protect anyone, it contradicts the contract's intent to ensure that the original intent of the contract is followed by the parties involved and potentially undermining the contract's enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Severability. All agreements and covenants contained herein are severable. If any of them, or any part or parts of them, shall be held invalid by any court of competent jurisdiction for any reason, then the Member agrees that the court shall have the authority to reform and modify that provision at their discretion. In so doing, the Member agrees that the court shall impose the provision with retroactive effect if needed.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the court to reform and modify invalid provisions to protect the Franchisor's and Franchisee's legitimate business needs. The changed text allows the court to reform and modify the provision at "their discretion", without needing to protect anyone. This contradicts the initial intent of protecting the Franchisor and Franchisee's business needs as explicitly stated, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text in this scenario, which is allowed as per the instructions.
